programme.title: Murder Trial: The Suffolk Strangler
programme.desc: On 3rd February 2026, former forklift driver Steve Wright pleaded guilty to the murder of Victoria Hall, 27 years after the 17-year-old was found dead after going missing in 1999. Over a two-week period in December 2006, five sex workers were found dead, their bodies dumped in rural locations outside Ipswich. Suffolk police launched their biggest ever manhunt, for a suspected serial killer on the loose - and on the rampage. A DNA match led police to Steve Wright, who would go on to be known as the Suffolk Strangler, yet there's a side to this story that you've not heard before. In this feature-length drama-documentary we go inside the courtroom. With privileged access to both the prosecution and to Wright's defence, for the first time we reveal their battle in the courtroom. And we examine the crucial evidence of the forensic experts, who played a major role in the outcome of the trial. Prod/Dir: Tom Harrad; Exec Prod: Elliot Reed; Good TV
